skill.name: gws-keep
skill.description: Manage Google Keep notes with full CRUD operations for notes, attachments, and permissions through the official Google Workspace CLI.
skill.summary: Google Keep notes management via gws CLI
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: All 24 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is a documentation wrapper for the official Google Workspace CLI (gws) tool. No actual shell commands, path traversal vulnerabilities, or cryptographic algorithms exist in this documentation file. The backtick patterns detected are markdown code formatting. The relative path reference (../gws-shared/SKILL.md) is a documentation link to another skill file.

content.user_title: Manage Google Keep notes with gws CLI
content.value_statement: Need to create, list, or delete Google Keep notes programmatically? This skill provides Claude Code with the full Google Keep API surface through the official gws CLI tool.
actual_capabilities: Create new Google Keep notes with title and content
List all notes with pagination support using page tokens
Get individual note details by ID
Download note attachments in available MIME types
limitations: Requires gws CLI binary installed on the system
User must have authenticated Google Workspace credentials
Cannot access notes shared without OWNER or Viewer permissions

use_cases.title: Automate note-taking for meetings
use_cases.description: Create structured notes automatically after meetings by extracting action items and saving them to Google Keep for team visibility.

use_cases.title: Backup important notes regularly
use_cases.description: Export notes to local storage for backup purposes or migration to other note-taking systems.
use_cases.target_user: Knowledge workers with critical information in Keep

use_cases.title: Sync notes across projects
use_cases.description: List all notes across your organization to find and consolidate related information scattered across multiple Keep notes.

prompt_templates.title: List all my Google Keep notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the gws CLI to list my Google Keep notes. Run `gws keep notes list` with appropriate pagination flags to show all available notes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a note with specific content
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Google Keep note titled "Meeting Summary - [Date]" with the following content: [paste content]. Use `gws keep notes create` with --params or --json flags.

prompt_templates.title: Download attachment from a note
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the note with ID [NOTE_ID] and download any attachments. Use `gws keep media download` with appropriate parameters. Verify the MIME type is supported before downloading.

prompt_templates.title: Batch note management with error handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Delete notes that match the pattern "Old - [Date]". First list notes with `gws keep notes list`, then use the returned IDs to delete each one. Handle rate limits and ensure you have OWNER permissions on each note before deletion.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - batch operations with permission checks

output_examples.output: Listed notes with pagination tokens. Each note shows ID, title, and last modified timestamp.

output_examples.input: gws keep notes create --json '{"title": "Q4 Planning", "content": "Discuss budget allocations"}'
output_examples.output: Note created successfully with a new note ID. Returns the full note object including created timestamp.
Note deleted immediately. No recovery possible. Confirmation message indicates permanent removal.

best_practices: Always use `gws schema keep.<resource>.<method>` to inspect required parameters before making API calls
Implement pagination handling when listing notes to avoid missing results beyond the first page
Verify OWNER permissions before deleting notes to prevent authorization errors
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to modify notes without checking your permission level first
Avoid downloading attachments without checking MIME type availability first
Do not hardcode note IDs in scripts without validation checks

faq.question: What authentication does this skill require?
faq.answer: You need Google Workspace credentials configured via the gws CLI. Run `gws auth login` to set up authentication before using Keep commands.

faq.question: Can I edit existing notes with this skill?
faq.answer: The current skill documentation only lists create, delete, get, and list operations. Edit operations may be available in the gws CLI but are not documented in this skill version.

faq.question: How do I find the correct note ID?
faq.answer: Use `gws keep notes list` to retrieve notes with their IDs. Each note in the response includes a unique identifier you can use for get, delete, or permission operations.

faq.question: What happens if I delete a note I do not own?
faq.answer: The gws CLI will return an authorization error. Only notes where you have the OWNER role can be deleted.

faq.question: How do I handle pagination for large note collections?
faq.answer: Use the page_token from ListNotesResponse to fetch subsequent pages. Set page_size to control results per page, or set it to zero to let the server choose.

faq.question: Can I share notes with team members?
faq.answer: Yes, use the `gws keep notes permissions` resource to manage note sharing. Ensure recipients have appropriate Google Workspace access.
